status: open

blocked_by: [oc-49555c7, oc-2fbcd7b, oc-9949561, oc-fc348f0]

# S1 holds — repos on my infrastructure, never losable

Root outcome for the current phase. Definition of done: every box in the acceptance checklist at [user-scenarios.md § S1](../../design/user-scenarios.md#s1--my-repos-live-on-my-infrastructure-and-i-can-never-lose-them) is checked — by looking, not by reviewing configuration.

input.description: Check blocked_by semantics in the idea docs

content.content:
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-3145b7a-demand-pressure.md-21-
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-3145b7a-demand-pressure.md-22-Stated plainly, the consequence: the priority-layer question that [[ida-eac723e]] split in two — **work-scheduling** vs **attention-routing** — partially *reunifies*. Attention-routing is work-scheduling for the actor class that cannot be spawned. A human-blocked outcome is not a different kind of thing from an agent-dispatchable one; it is a frontier node whose assigned actor happens to be reached by notification rather than by process creation. (The firehose half of attention-routing — what a human must *see* across all finished work — stays a separate open question; only the "human as blocking producer" half folds back in.)
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-3145b7a-demand-pressure.md-23-
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-3145b7a-demand-pressure.md-24-## Pressure is standing, not event-shaped
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-3145b7a-demand-pressure.md-25-
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-3145b7a-demand-pressure.md:26:Demand propagates from live root outcomes down the `blocked_by` closure, exactly as priority does in the adopted sketch — but the controller does not dispatch once and forget. It continuously reconciles *what should be moving but isn't*: level-triggered, not edge-triggered, the same reconcile-don't-just-react controller principle the rest of the architecture leans on. An event kicks a recompute sooner; the absence of events never means the absence of pressure.
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-3145b7a-demand-pressure.md-27-
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-3145b7a-demand-pressure.md-28-## Stall is the named failure mode

/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-3145b7a-demand-pressure.md-31-
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-17-

/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-20-Nothing new needs to be stored. The dependency structure is already in the typed-node knowledge graph (PR #1's `knowledge.md`; thin form in [architecture.md § project knowledge is a typed-node graph](../../design/architecture.md#bet-project-knowledge-is-a-typed-node-graph)):
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-21-
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md:22:- `blocked_by` / `blocks` edges between outcome nodes **are** a dependency DAG. An outcome is `blocked_by` the sub-outcomes that must complete before it can.
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-23-- `closes` + the attestation-success → `node-status-changed` event is exactly how an outcome **finalizes** a node: a change lands carrying `closes: [oc-…]`, the integrator's success event flips the node's status, and the edge that blocked its parent is gone.
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-24-
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-25-The descriptive reading — "here is what blocks what" — is already supported. The new move is to read the same graph **generatively**: the open outcomes are not a record of intent, they are a worklist the system is under pressure to discharge. Every unfinished outcome is latent demand for an agent run.

/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-36-       oc-auth-bug-fixed   ← frontier (no open blockers)
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-37-```
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-38-
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-39-## Priority propagates; the frontier gets scheduled
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-40-
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md:41:Root outcomes — the things a human or another system actually asked for — carry a **priority**. Priority is not a property of every node; it is set at the roots and **propagates down the DAG** to the outcomes that must complete for that root to complete (its transitive `blocked_by` closure — the ancestors-of-completion). A sub-outcome inherits the highest priority among the roots that depend on it.
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-42-
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-43-Under contention, the scheduler dispatches the **frontier**: the unblocked outcomes (no open blockers remaining) that lie on the critical path to the highest-priority root. Producing the nodes that finalize the DAG for a high-priority root *is* the scheduling mechanism — there is no separate queue of "work to do," only the graph and the pressure to complete it.
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-44-
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-45-This is **dependency-aware, critical-path scheduling**, not an attention router. It answers "what should the system work on next," not "what should a human look at." Those are different problems, and conflating them was the mistake the feedback reframe ([architecture.md § review is observability + feedback](../../design/architecture.md#bet-review-is-observability--feedback)) left half-made — see *What this splits* below.
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-46-
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-47-| Concept | Meaning |
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-48-| --- | --- |
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-49-| Root outcome | An outcome with no parent in the DAG; carries an explicit priority |
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md:50:| Priority propagation | A root's priority flows along `blocked_by` to every ancestor-of-completion |
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-51-| Frontier | Unblocked outcomes whose blockers are all closed; eligible to dispatch |
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-52-| Critical path | The chain of frontier→…→root that gates a given root's completion |
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-53-| Finalization | `closes` + attestation-success → `node-status-changed`; removes a blocking edge |
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-54-
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-55-## Dispatch is klaus-shaped

/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-36-       oc-auth-bug-fixed   ← frontier (no open blockers)
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-37-```
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-38-
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-39-## Priority propagates; the frontier gets scheduled
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-40-
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md:41:Root outcomes — the things a human or another system actually asked for — carry a **priority**. Priority is not a property of every node; it is set at the roots and **propagates down the DAG** to the outcomes that must complete for that root to complete (its transitive `blocked_by` closure — the ancestors-of-completion). A sub-outcome inherits the highest priority among the roots that depend on it.
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-42-
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-43-Under contention, the scheduler dispatches the **frontier**: the unblocked outcomes (no open blockers remaining) that lie on the critical path to the highest-priority root. Producing the nodes that finalize the DAG for a high-priority root *is* the scheduling mechanism — there is no separate queue of "work to do," only the graph and the pressure to complete it.
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-44-
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-45-This is **dependency-aware, critical-path scheduling**, not an attention router. It answers "what should the system work on next," not "what should a human look at." Those are different problems, and conflating them was the mistake the feedback reframe ([architecture.md § review is observability + feedback](../../design/architecture.md#bet-review-is-observability--feedback)) left half-made — see *What this splits* below.
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-46-
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-47-| Concept | Meaning |
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-48-| --- | --- |
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-49-| Root outcome | An outcome with no parent in the DAG; carries an explicit priority |
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md:50:| Priority propagation | A root's priority flows along `blocked_by` to every ancestor-of-completion |
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-51-| Frontier | Unblocked outcomes whose blockers are all closed; eligible to dispatch |
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-52-| Critical path | The chain of frontier→…→root that gates a given root's completion |
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-53-| Finalization | `closes` + attestation-success → `node-status-changed`; removes a blocking edge |
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-54-
/tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260702-1154-60315adf/.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md-55-## Dispatch is klaus-shaped

content.text:
All done. PR #7 had one review comment, and it was valid — I applied it, pushed, and replied.

**What changed:** In `.the-valley/outcomes/oc-f3bcfd0-s1-holds.md`, the root outcome listed four blockers, but two of them (`oc-49555c7` and `oc-fc348f0`) were already reachable transitively through the other two (`oc-2fbcd7b` is blocked by `oc-49555c7`; `oc-9949561` by `oc-fc348f0`). I verified this against the actual `blocked_by` lines in all six outcome files, and confirmed the repo's own docs (`ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md`) define priority propagation over the *transitive* `blocked_by` closure — so the reduction to `[oc-2fbcd7b, oc-9949561]` is semantically lossless. Nothing else in the repo referenced the full blocker list. Committed as `38caa6e` and pushed; replied to the comment.

**One caveat you should know:** PR #7 was already **squash-merged** into main (as `5a23117`) before this run, and its remote branch had been deleted. My push recreated `agent/20260702-1150-e3712f77` with the fix on top of the old head, but since the PR is closed, the commit will not flow into main on its own. I was instructed not to open a new PR, so the fix currently lives only on that branch — it needs a follow-up PR (or a cherry-pick of `38caa6e`) to actually land. I noted this in the review-comment reply as well.

result:
All done. PR #7 had one review comment, and it was valid — I applied it, pushed, and replied.

**What changed:** In `.the-valley/outcomes/oc-f3bcfd0-s1-holds.md`, the root outcome listed four blockers, but two of them (`oc-49555c7` and `oc-fc348f0`) were already reachable transitively through the other two (`oc-2fbcd7b` is blocked by `oc-49555c7`; `oc-9949561` by `oc-fc348f0`). I verified this against the actual `blocked_by` lines in all six outcome files, and confirmed the repo's own docs (`ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md`) define priority propagation over the *transitive* `blocked_by` closure — so the reduction to `[oc-2fbcd7b, oc-9949561]` is semantically lossless. Nothing else in the repo referenced the full blocker list. Committed as `38caa6e` and pushed; replied to the comment.

**One caveat you should know:** PR #7 was already **squash-merged** into main (as `5a23117`) before this run, and its remote branch had been deleted. My push recreated `agent/20260702-1150-e3712f77` with the fix on top of the old head, but since the PR is closed, the commit will not flow into main on its own. I was instructed not to open a new PR, so the fix currently lives only on that branch — it needs a follow-up PR (or a cherry-pick of `38caa6e`) to actually land. I noted this in the review-comment reply as well.
